title: Transitioning to Post-Quantum Cryptography (joint statement)
summary: Joint statement hosted and co-signed by BSI on transitioning to post-quantum cryptography, noting that several well-studied PQC alternatives to currently deployed vulnerable cryptography are available.

title: Transition deadlines agreed for post-quantum cryptography in classified IT (VS-IT)
summary: BSI announcement that deadlines have been agreed for migrating German classified IT systems (VS-IT) to post-quantum cryptography.

title: Cooperation group publishes EU roadmap for quantum-safe cryptography (press release)
summary: BSI press release on the publication of an EU roadmap for quantum-safe cryptography, prepared by a working group on post-quantum cryptography established in 2024.

title: BSI and partners from 17 other EU member states demand transition to post-quantum cryptography
summary: BSI press release announcing that BSI and partner agencies from 17 other EU member states jointly call for the transition to post-quantum cryptography.

title: Post-quantum cryptography (topic page)
summary: BSI information page explaining post-quantum cryptography as cryptographic schemes assumed to remain unbreakable even with the help of a quantum computer.

title: Quantum technologies and quantum-safe cryptography (topic page)
summary: BSI topic page on quantum technologies and quantum-safe cryptography, referencing the BSI guideline 'Quantum-safe cryptography - fundamentals, current developments and recommendations'.

title: Position paper on quantum key distribution
summary: Position paper on quantum key distribution published by BSI under its quantum technologies and quantum-safe cryptography workstream.

title: BSI TR-02102-1 Cryptographic mechanisms: recommendations and key lengths
summary: Technical Guideline in which BSI provides an assessment of cryptographic mechanisms and key lengths; recent updates extend the recommendations to post-quantum cryptography.

title: Government plans roadmap for post-quantum cryptography
summary: Bundestag news item reporting that the Federal Government plans a roadmap for post-quantum cryptography and supports implementation through the funding directive 'Bringing post-quantum cryptography into applications'.

title: Bundestag printed paper 21/2526 on the transition to quantum-safe cryptography
summary: Bundestag printed paper stating that a national project plan for the transition to quantum-safe cryptography is currently being drawn up in line with the relevant recommendation.

title: Bundestag printed paper 20/8104 on post-quantum cryptography readiness
summary: Bundestag printed paper stating that Germany is well positioned on post-quantum cryptography and that the BSI issued early recommendations for the migration to PQC.

title: Bundesrat session 1065 explanatory note on PQC migration in national cybersecurity strategies
summary: Explanatory note for the Bundesrat's 1065th session concerning an obligation for member states to adopt concepts for the migration to post-quantum cryptography (PQC) within their national cybersecurity strategies.

title: Bundesrat printed paper 166/24 on post-quantum cryptography
summary: Bundesrat printed paper describing post-quantum cryptography (PQC) as a promising approach and referencing the BSI's recommendations for action on post-quantum cryptography.

title: Looking ahead: how will quantum computing change banking? (speech)
summary: Bundesbank speech on quantum computing in banking, noting the Federal Government's goal of initiating the migration to post-quantum cryptography in security-critical areas by 2026.

title: Bringing post-quantum cryptography into applications (funding call)
summary: BMBF funding announcement for projects on the development and analysis of cryptographic schemes that withstand quantum computers, aimed at bringing post-quantum cryptography into application.

title: Federal Government action plan for quantum technologies (Handlungskonzept)
summary: Federal Government action plan for quantum technologies, which addresses post-quantum cryptography as an important element in the quantum technology context, noting it is implemented on classical hardware.

title: Agenda for cybersecurity research
summary: BMBF cybersecurity research agenda that includes researching post-quantum cryptography and bringing it into broad application to be prepared for the future era of quantum computers.

title: Research programme quantum systems
summary: BMBF federal research programme on quantum systems that covers post-quantum cryptography (PQK) among the topics addressed in the programme.

title: Digital. Secure. Sovereign. (IT security research framework programme)
summary: Federal research framework programme for IT security noting that diverging international developments are expected for new algorithms such as post-quantum cryptography.

title: Federal Report on Research and Innovation 2020
summary: Federal Government report on research and innovation referencing post-quantum cryptography, whose aim is to develop encryption schemes that remain secure in the era of quantum computers.

title: Quantum-secure communication (guideline)
summary: Guideline by the German digital industry association Bitkom on quantum-secure communication, addressing quantum-safe cryptography methods designed to withstand the capabilities of quantum computers. Industry association rather than a government body, flagged for review.

title: PQC update from BSI: policies, activities and roadmap (presentation)
summary: BSI conference presentation outlining the agency's PQC policies, activities and roadmap, including continued migration to post-quantum cryptography for high-security applications. Hosted on a conference platform rather than an official BSI domain, flagged for review.

title: BSI TR-02102-2 Cryptographic mechanisms: recommendations on TLS
summary: BSI Technical Guideline with recommendations for the use of the Transport Layer Security (TLS) protocol, part of the TR-02102 series of cryptographic recommendations. Explicit PQC reference not visible in the candidate snippet, flagged for review.

title: Federal Quantum Alliance (Bundesquantenallianz) resources
summary: Resource page of the Bundesquantenallianz, a cooperation of federally owned companies preparing for the age of quantum computing. Explicit PQC reference not visible in the candidate snippet, flagged for review.

title: Bundestag printed paper on the PQC roadmap (DIP full text)
summary: Machine-readable full text of a Bundestag printed paper discussing the presentation of a roadmap for post-quantum cryptography in Germany and the promotion of migration to quantum-safe cryptography. May duplicate one of the included Drucksachen, flagged for review.

title: BSI empfiehlt Ende klassischer asymmetrischer Verschlüsselungsverfahren
summary: BSI press release (11 February 2026) announcing that, in its annual update of the cryptographic recommendations (TR-02102), the BSI for the first time sets end-of-life dates for classical asymmetric encryption (no sole use after end-2031, end-2030 for highly sensitive applications; classical signatures by end-2035), to be replaced by hybrid post-quantum cryptography. BSI President Plattner states the transition to post-quantum cryptography is 'alternativlos' (without alternative).

title: BSI erweitert kryptographische Empfehlungen mit Blick auf Quantencomputer
summary: BSI press release (10 March 2025) announcing an update to the TR-02102 technical guideline that extends the cryptographic recommendations to address the quantum-computer threat, recommending post-quantum algorithms (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, FrodoKEM, Classic McEliece, XMSS, LMS) and migration of high-security applications by 2030 in coordination with 17 European partners.
